steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject is "Mathematician Grigori Perelman" — a single person, therefore singular. The long phrase "sometimes in conjunction with mathematicians Richard S. Hamilton and Shing-Tung Yau" is a parenthetical modifier, not part of the subject. The verb must agree with the singular subject Perelman.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'are': plural present verb; does not agree with the singular subject "Mathematician Grigori Perelman." ✗
  • Choice B — 'have been': plural present-perfect verb; does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — 'are being': plural present-progressive verb; does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'is': singular present verb; agrees with the singular subject "Mathematician Grigori Perelman." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
